behaviors.description: Storing a PASS review summary is blocked when any stored dimension finding for the same round has a FAIL verdict.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is the handoff between summary-write logic and persisted per-dimension findings. A unit test of only summary formatting would miss the cross-module consistency check. No real OS/network/subprocess is required, and no model-output quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Run summary persistence through the service/repository path with stored findings for the same round containing at least one FAIL; assert PASS summary write is rejected. Rejected shortcut: choosing Unit just because mocks exist would miss repository-state coupling.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is required because the failure appears at data handoff between modules; higher levels are not required by behavior text.

behaviors.description: Stop-gate verdict computation reads per-dimension stored findings directly and does not rely only on hand-written summary text.
behaviors.justification: The bug is source-of-truth selection across modules (gate evaluator vs stored findings vs summary text). Unit tests around a pure function can miss wiring errors where gate reads the wrong store/field. No real infra or LLM dependence is required.
behaviors.test_description: Seed stored findings with a FAIL while summary text claims PASS; execute stop-gate computation via real module wiring and assert verdict is not PASS and reflects stored findings. Rejected shortcut: defaulting to Integration for all multi-module features is wrong, but here the behavior text explicitly targets module boundary data sourcing.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is the minimum that proves gate reads persisted findings instead of summary text.

behaviors.description: Manual summary text overrides require an explicit override flag and produce an auditable warning record.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears when override authorization and audit recording are wired together. Unit tests on flag parsing alone miss missing/incorrect audit persistence. No real OS/network/subprocess or model judgment is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Submit manual summary override without flag and assert rejection; submit with flag and assert warning/audit record is written with actor, round, and reason fields. Rejected shortcut: choosing Unit because each rule is simple would miss the audit side-effect boundary.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test requires service + audit store interaction; not System/Agentic.

behaviors.description: After Phase 4 fixes, the gate requires a review round identifier produced after the latest fix commit before PASS can be finalized.
behaviors.justification: This is freshness/orchestration logic across commit metadata and review-round records. It needs module wiring (fix commit tracker + review-round store + gate), but not necessarily real git subprocess behavior. No LLM-output quality dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Create state where latest fix commit is newer than last review round; assert PASS finalization is blocked until a newer round id exists, then allowed. Rejected shortcut: escalating to System only because commits are involved; behavior text requires ordering logic, not real OS git semantics.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration covers the real failure boundary unless behavior explicitly required real git/process behavior.

behaviors.description: In the live review loop, PASS cannot finalize unless a fresh review-agent round runs on the updated diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires a live review-agent round on updated diff. Mocked agent calls can hide failures in real agent-run triggering and round creation under live conditions. This is a single agentic step (not multi-step workflow) because one fresh round is the required boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Run the live loop with an updated diff and attempt PASS finalization before and after an actual review-agent invocation; assert PASS only possible after a fresh post-update round is recorded. Rejected shortcut: keeping Integration by default for orchestration around LLM-involved stages; behavior text requires live review-agent execution.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Higher level Workflow is disqualified because behavior requires one fresh agentic round, not a multi-agentic chain.

behaviors.description: If review rounds are exhausted with unresolved MUST-FIX findings in stored results, the outcome is escalation rather than PASS.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is policy evaluation over persisted findings + round counters. Needs module wiring to ensure stored MUST-FIX results are read and escalation path is emitted. No real OS/network/subprocess and no model-output quality dependency in this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Seed stored results with unresolved MUST-FIX and round budget exhausted; run gate decision path and assert escalation state, never PASS. Rejected shortcut: choosing Unit because rule is simple; persisted-state retrieval and decision wiring are part of the failure boundary.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Integration is enough; Agentic/Workflow not required because behavior references stored results, not live model decisions.

behaviors.description: In a live multi-round review workflow, exhausted rounds with unresolved MUST-FIX findings still block PASS and emit escalation.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly chains multiple live review rounds and terminal gating behavior. Real failure can emerge across sequential agentic handoffs (round N outputs, carryover to round N+1, exhaustion decision). Single-step Agentic or Integration tests can miss cross-round state propagation defects.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a live multi-round sequence where MUST-FIX remains unresolved through all allowed rounds; verify final state blocks PASS and emits escalation artifact/event. Rejected shortcut: choosing Agentic for any LLM involvement; behavior text requires multi-round sequence, so Workflow is the minimum.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Workflow is required by explicit multi-round live sequence; lower levels would not exercise chained handoffs.
